metadata.description: Maps each deduplicated threat to its discovering Phase B specialists for confidence analysis

TECH-H001.title: initializeAlphaIndices Access Control Mismatch Permanently Blocks Generation Increment
TECH-H001.duplicate_group_description: initializeAlphaIndices on EntropyGenerator has onlyOwner modifier but is called by TraitForgeNft._incrementGeneration, which is allowedCaller not owner, permanently blocking generation advancement after 10,000 mints

ECON-H001.title: Fully Predictable Entropy Enables Selective High-Value Token Minting for Maximum NukeFund Extraction
ECON-H001.duplicate_group_description: Entropy values are deterministic, stored once, and publicly readable via getPublicEntropy, allowing any user to predict and selectively mint high-value tokens

TECH-H003.title: mintWithBudget Permanently Non-Functional After Generation 1
TECH-H003.duplicate_group_description: mintWithBudget uses global _tokenIds counter instead of generationMintCounts in its while-loop condition, making the function permanently non-functional after generation 1

TECH-H004.title: Forger Off-by-One Allows One Extra Forge Beyond Intended Limit
TECH-H004.duplicate_group_description: Off-by-one error in listForForging uses <= instead of < when comparing forgingCounts against forgePotential, allowing every forger one extra forge beyond intended limit

OPS-H002.title: Emergency Pause Mechanism Completely Non-Functional Across All Contracts
OPS-H002.duplicate_group_description: All contracts inherit Pausable and use whenNotPaused modifiers but none expose external pause()/unpause() functions, making the emergency stop mechanism completely non-functional

TECH-H006.title: Entropy Slot Recycling Makes All Post-Gen-1 Token Traits Predictable and Repeated
TECH-H006.duplicate_group_description: Entropy slots are never regenerated across generations; getNextEntropy wraps currentSlotIndex to 0 after 10,010 values, causing identical entropy sequence reuse in all subsequent generations

TECH-H007.title: Excess ETH Permanently Locked in EntityForging Contract
TECH-H007.duplicate_group_description: forgeWithListed checks msg.value >= forgingFee but does not refund excess ETH, permanently locking overpayments in EntityForging which has no withdrawal mechanism

TECH-H008.title: Stale listedTokenIds After Cancellation Causes Incorrect Forging Listing Behavior
TECH-H008.duplicate_group_description: EntityForging._cancelListingForForging deletes the listing struct but does not clear listedTokenIds[tokenId], leaving stale pointers that cause state confusion and potential transfer DoS via _beforeTokenTransfer interactions

TECH-H009.title: Generation Counter Can Exceed maxGeneration Via _incrementGeneration
TECH-H009.duplicate_group_description: _incrementGeneration increments currentGeneration without checking against maxGeneration, allowing minting beyond the intended 10-generation cap

ECON-H007.title: No Access Control on writeEntropyBatch Functions Allows Adversarial Entropy Seeding
ECON-H007.duplicate_group_description: writeEntropyBatch1/2/3 are public functions with no access control, allowing anyone to call them and control the entropy sequence by choosing the block.number at write time

ECON-H003.title: Uninitialized ageMultiplier Nullifies Age-Based Nuke Factor Making NukeFund Exploitable Immediately
ECON-H003.duplicate_group_description: NukeFund.ageMultiplier defaults to 0 and is never set in deployment, causing calculateAge to always return 0 and nullifying the hold-to-earn mechanic

TECH-H012.title: NukeFund receive() DoS via ETH Transfer Failure to devAddress/daoAddress
TECH-H012.duplicate_group_description: NukeFund.receive() and DevFund.receive() use require(success) on ETH transfers to devAddress/owner/daoAddress; if any recipient rejects ETH, all minting/trading/forging is permanently bricked

TECH-H013.title: Forging listing cancellation can permanently brick ALL NFT transfers if EntityForging is unset or miswired
TECH-H013.duplicate_group_description: TraitForgeNft._beforeTokenTransfer makes external calls to entityForgingContract that can revert if the contract is unset, misconfigured, or malfunctioning, blocking all NFT transfers including mints, burns, and trades

TECH-H015.title: EntityTrading listings can be overwritten/relisted without clearing listedTokenIds, enabling stale-index state and unexpected behavior
TECH-H015.duplicate_group_description: EntityTrading deletes listing structs on buy/cancel but never clears listedTokenIds mapping, creating stale pointers that can cause listing corruption and asset lockout

ECON-H014.title: Mint Price Plummets on Generation Increment Enabling Arbitrage
ECON-H014.duplicate_group_description: Mint price drops ~98% when a new generation starts because generationMintCounts resets to 0, creating a massive arbitrage opportunity at generation boundaries

OPS-H017.title: NukeFund claim cap can be bypassed by parameter combination (nukeFactorMaxParam vs maxAllowedClaimDivisor) leading to >50% fund claims
OPS-H017.duplicate_group_description: NukeFund.nuke() applies the claim cap only when finalNukeFactor > nukeFactorMaxParam instead of using min(potentialClaim, maxClaim), allowing claims to exceed intended maximum when parameters are misconfigured or finalNukeFactor is high but below threshold

ECON2-H020.title: Airdrop Contract Failure in burn() Can Permanently Lock NukeFund ETH
ECON2-H020.duplicate_group_description: TraitForgeNft.burn() makes unchecked external calls to airdropContract; if airdropContract is unset, misconfigured, or reverts, all burns and nuke claims are permanently blocked, locking NukeFund ETH

ECON2-H018.title: DevFund Integer Division Truncation Routes Small Payments Entirely to Owner
ECON2-H018.duplicate_group_description: DevFund.receive() integer division truncation causes amountPerWeight=0 when msg.value < totalDevWeight, routing the entire payment to owner as remainder instead of to developers
